topics.tldr: A quiet week for major reporting or analysis on AI crawlers and publisher controls, with most recent activity appearing to be older than the strict 14-day recency requirement.

This week's analysis highlights growing concerns over AI agent security, control, and the high potential for enterprise deployment failures, alongside discussions on critical infrastructure needs and the challenges of debugging non-deterministic agent behavior.

items.title: The Agentic Blockchain Stack: How AI Agents Are Rewriting On-Chain Infrastructure in 2026
items.frame: AI agents are fundamentally changing blockchain infrastructure requirements, demanding real-time, multi-chain data and event-driven pipelines for autonomous operations.
items.quote: "As we move deeper into 2026, one truth is becoming undeniable: the infrastructure powering these agents determines whether they win or fail."

topics.tldr: This week's analysis highlights escalating generative AI copyright lawsuits challenging fair use, new EU regulations increasing liability for open-source AI, and calls for stronger copyright protection in Japan for AI-generated animation.

items.title: How AI and new regulations are shaping open source in 2026
items.frame: New EU regulations like the Cyber Resilience Act are increasing legal liability for companies using open-source AI components, emphasizing licensing integrity and security.
items.quote: The European Union Cyber Resilience Act (EU CRA) is increasing obligations throughout 2026. It fundamentally changes liability, making companies legally accountable for the security of open-source components in their products.

topics.tldr: A quiet week; most activity flowed through the daily feed, with no substantive reporting or analysis on AI's impact on the web ecosystem, publisher traffic, or content licensing from major outlets in the last 14 days.
